data.description:
## Context

Split out of the code-grounding T2 epic (`850f-bf87-8600-4af6`, ADR 0030). Terraform is **not** a
code-build environment — it declares cloud infrastructure and provides no language server or dependency
resolution — so it is out of scope as a "build env for code resolution." But IaC findings have their
**own** tiered grounding that mirrors the T0/T1/T2 model in a different domain. The general lesson from
ADR 0030 is that the tiering **recurs per domain**; IaC is a distinct grounding **lane**, captured here
as its own epic rather than folded into `850f`.

## Success Criteria

- An IaC grounding lane exists that emits the same three-valued, fail-open evidence contract
  (`refuted` / `match` / `abstain`, closed reasons, provenance tier) as the code lane, with no changes
  to the contract or its consumers.
- IaC resolution is confirm-only and off any deterministic blocking path unless a tier is proven
  trustworthy (offline `terraform validate` may qualify; live `terraform plan` never does by default).

## Context — the IaC tiering (to be designed)

- **IaC T0 — Registry existence.** Terraform Registry provider/module existence probe (the analogue of
  the code T0 deps-existence lane): a declared provider/module that does not exist in the Registry is a
  refutable reference.
- **IaC T1 — offline reference resolution.** `terraform validate` (no cloud creds, no network to a
  provider API): validates that references between resources/variables/outputs resolve within the
  configuration. Offline + deterministic-ish → the IaC analogue of the T1 syntactic floor.
- **IaC T2 — live plan.** `terraform plan` (needs cloud credentials + provider API access): the
  high-fidelity, non-deterministic, credential-bearing tier — the analogue of code T2, and subject to
  the same confirm-only + never-on-a-blocking-path constraints (plus a credential-handling/security
  design, since `plan` authenticates to a cloud account).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Decide the v1 IaC tier(s) to implement among Registry existence (T0), `terraform validate` (T1),
      and `terraform plan` (T2), with the determinism/credential/security tradeoffs recorded in an ADR.
- [ ] The chosen tier(s) emit the existing three-valued evidence contract at the correct provenance
      tier, with no change to the contract or its consumers.
- [ ] IaC resolution is self-contained + opt-in (gated behind config / an optional extra) and
      fail-open (missing `terraform` binary / no creds / timeout → `abstain` with a closed reason).
- [ ] `terraform plan` (if in v1 scope) never runs on a deterministic blocking path and never carries
      credentials into an untrusted context; otherwise it is explicitly deferred with rationale.
- [ ] Fail-open matrix tested per implemented tier.
data.title: IaC grounding lane: tiered terraform validate/Registry/plan resolution (T0/T1/T2), split from code-grounding T2
